plan.objective: Add a `verify_lock(path) -> Result<Option<Vec<LockVerifyEntry>>>` function plus `LockVerifyStatus` enum and `LockVerifyEntry` struct to `core/src/registry.rs`, enabling grove doctor (T07) to recompute each cached grammar wasm's sha256 and compare it against the lock file.
key_changes: New `pub enum LockVerifyStatus { Match, Mismatch, Missing }` in registry.rs
New `pub struct LockVerifyEntry { lang, expected, actual: Option<String>, status }` in registry.rs
New `pub fn verify_lock(path: &Path) -> Result<Option<Vec<LockVerifyEntry>>>` — Ok(None) when lock absent, Ok(Some(vec)) when parsed
Wasm resolved via `registry_root()/<name>/grammar.wasm` (same path as `resolve()`; covers dev-stubs, cache_root, GROVE_REGISTRY)
NotFound error on wasm path → Missing; hash mismatch → Mismatch; equal hashes → Match
Reuses existing `sha256()` primitive so a lock written by `write_lock_for` verifies clean
Four unit tests: absent-lock (Ok(None)), Match after write_lock_for, Mismatch on tampered hash, Missing on nonexistent lang wasm
Add `pub use registry::{LockVerifyEntry, LockVerifyStatus}` to core/src/lib.rs curated surface
No new files, no Cargo.toml changes, no lock-format changes — purely additive
Not material: additive read-only primitive, no CLI surface, no version bump required

review_plan.objective: Assess the plan to add a read-only grove.lock wasm sha256 verify primitive (verify_lock + LockVerifyStatus/LockVerifyEntry) to core/src/registry.rs for grove doctor (T07).
key_changes: Verified against actual code: lock JSON shape {version,grammars:[{name,version,wasm}]} matches write_lock_for@412; plan's parse of doc[grammars]/entry[wasm]/name is correct.
AC#2 holds: wasm_sha256@106 == sha256(self.wasm) and verify_lock recomputes sha256 over the same file bytes, so a fresh lock verifies all-Match.
Wasm path registry_root()/<name>/grammar.wasm equals resolve@219's idx.root.join(lang)/grammar.wasm in steady state (index root seeded from registry_root).
Missing/Mismatch/None signals map correctly to NotFound / hash-diff / absent-file; Ok(None) is a clean non-error signal (AC#3).
Change is purely additive (new public types+fn, no signature/lock-format change) — matches 'no version bump' operational impact.
Advisory: index() memoizes root via OnceLock while verify_lock re-evaluates registry_root(); keep the Match test registry-root-agnostic (no mid-process GROVE_REGISTRY mutation) to avoid divergence.
Advisory: decide malformed-entry handling (entry missing wasm/name) deliberately; avoid silent unwrap.
Advisory: verify_lock takes the lock file path (like locked_langs), not a project root — T07 must pass the resolved grove.lock path.
All five acceptance criteria addressed; no changes required before implementation.
findings: See key_changes — all findings mirrored there for schema compliance.

implementation.objective: Add verify_lock(path) -> Result<Option<Vec<LockVerifyEntry>>> plus LockVerifyStatus enum and LockVerifyEntry struct to core/src/registry.rs, enabling grove doctor (T07) to recompute each cached grammar wasm's sha256 and compare it against the lock file.
key_changes: Added pub enum LockVerifyStatus { Match, Mismatch, Missing } to registry.rs
Added pub struct LockVerifyEntry { lang, expected, actual: Option<String>, status } to registry.rs
Added pub fn verify_lock(path: &Path) -> Result<Option<Vec<LockVerifyEntry>>> — Ok(None) when lock absent
Wasm resolved via registry_root()/<name>/grammar.wasm (same path as resolve())
NotFound I/O error → Missing; hash equality → Match; hash mismatch → Mismatch
Other I/O errors propagated via anyhow with context
Added verify_lock_returns_none_when_file_absent unit test
Added verify_lock_matches_after_write_lock_for unit test (fresh lock verifies all Match)
Added verify_lock_detects_tampered_hash unit test (mutated wasm field → Mismatch)
Added verify_lock_detects_missing_wasm unit test (nonexistent lang → Missing)
Added pub use registry::{LockVerifyEntry, LockVerifyStatus} to core/src/lib.rs curated surface
All 165 unit tests pass; 4 new verify_lock tests all green

code_review.objective: Reviewed the additive verify_lock primitive plus LockVerifyStatus/LockVerifyEntry types in core/src/registry.rs and the lib.rs re-export against the approved plan.
key_changes: All 8 plan/AC items met: types, verify_lock signature, wasm path resolution, status mapping, hash-format consistency, curated re-export, purely additive.
Independently ran cargo test verify_lock: 4 passed, 0 failed (161 filtered = 165 total, matches claim).
cargo clippy -p grove-cst --release --locked: clean, zero warnings.
Ok(None) on absent lock; NotFound->Missing; hash-eq->Match; diff->Mismatch; other I/O errors propagated with anyhow context (no silent swallow).
Fresh lock verifies all-Match: verify_lock reuses sha256() and write_lock_for pins wasm_sha256() delegating to same primitive.
Malformed entries (missing name/wasm) are continue-skipped, not unwrapped — deliberate non-panicking choice per plan-review advisory.
Doc comment correctly directs T07 to pass the resolved grove.lock file path, not a project root.
Tests authentic and meaningful (round-trip Match, tampered->Mismatch, nonexistent->Missing, absent->None).
Advisory (non-blocking): lock top-level version field not validated; fine for read-only advisory primitive.
Advisory (non-blocking): temp-test files share process id but use distinct name stems; no collision.
findings: All 8 plan/AC items met and independently verified.
cargo test verify_lock: 4/4 pass; clippy clean.
Correct Match/Mismatch/Missing/None signal mapping; errors propagated not swallowed.
Additive-only: no signature, lock-format, or Cargo.toml changes.

validation.objective: Validated that verify_lock + LockVerifyStatus/LockVerifyEntry are correctly implemented in core/src/registry.rs against all five acceptance criteria.
key_changes: AC#1 PASS: verify_lock returns Ok(None) for absent lock file; test verify_lock_returns_none_when_file_absent passes.
AC#2 PASS: All three status variants (Match/Mismatch/Missing) tested and passing; assertions are specific.
AC#3 PASS: verify_lock calls sha256(&bytes); write_lock_for uses wasm_sha256() delegating to same sha256 — round-trip Match test confirms.
AC#4 PASS: Exactly 4 unit tests covering absent-lock, Match, Mismatch, Missing — all 4 pass.
AC#5 PASS: cargo clippy -D warnings clean; cargo test 287 total 0 failed; files end with newline.
Public surface: pub use registry::{LockVerifyEntry, LockVerifyStatus} confirmed at lib.rs:70.
Error handling: NotFound->Missing, other I/O errors propagated via anyhow, malformed entries skipped.
Additive only: no existing signatures, lock format, or Cargo.toml changed. No regressions.

approve.objective: Architect sign-off on the additive read-only verify_lock wasm sha256 primitive (LockVerifyStatus/LockVerifyEntry) in core/src/registry.rs for grove doctor (T07).
key_changes: Purely additive: no existing signatures, lock-file format, or Cargo.toml deps changed — nothing downstream must migrate.
Verifier reuses the same sha256() primitive as write_lock_for/wasm_sha256(), so a fresh lock round-trips to all-Match with no writer/verifier skew.
Wasm path registry_root()/<name>/grammar.wasm is consistent with resolve(); covers dev-stubs, cache_root, GROVE_REGISTRY.
Signal mapping clean and non-swallowing: Ok(None) absent, NotFound->Missing, eq->Match, diff->Mismatch; other I/O errors propagate via anyhow.
Malformed lock entries deliberately continue-skipped, not unwrapped — resolves the plan-review 'decide deliberately' advisory.
No cross-cutting coupling; doc comment correctly directs T07 to pass the resolved grove.lock file path, not a project root.
Independently corroborated: code review 8/8 plan/AC + clippy clean; validation 5/5 AC pass, full suite green.
Deployment: no version bump on its own, no regeneration, no migration, no security scan.
Advisory (non-blocking): lock top-level version field not validated — acceptable for a read-only advisory primitive; fold into T07 if doctor ever gates on schema version.
Follow-up: T07 grove doctor is the intended consumer — surface Mismatch/Missing as actionable diagnostics.
